name: Alibi Detect
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); from Seldon (commercial backing); no API costs

performance.notes: Online detectors add minimal overhead; offline/batch varies with algorithm and data size

agent_readiness.notes: Statistical outlier detection, adversarial detection, and data drift detection library. Algorithms: CUSUM, MMD, LSDD, Chi-squared, KS test, ClassifierDrift, Mahalanobis, VAE-based. Online (streaming) and offline (batch) modes. GPU acceleration for deep learning detectors. Used in Seldon Core serving infrastructure. Particularly strong for adversarial and out-of-distribution (OOD) detection.

gotchas: Higher learning curve than Evidently for basic drift detection
Some detectors require calibration period to establish reference distribution
TF/PyTorch dependency for deep learning-based detectors
Documentation can be inconsistent across algorithms
best_when: Adversarial attack detection in production ML serving
Statistical drift detection with rigorous algorithmic control
Online streaming drift detection for real-time systems
avoid_when: Simple data quality monitoring (Evidently or Great Expectations simpler)
Teams wanting turnkey dashboards without coding
summary: Alibi Detect is an open-source library for outlier detection, adversarial detection, and data drift detection in ML systems. Provides statistical algorithms (MMD, LSDD, KS, Chi-squared) and deep learning-based detectors. Supports online (streaming) and offline (batch) modes. Particularly strong for adversarial and out-of-distribution detection. Used in Seldon production ML infrastructure.
